title: mRemoteNg 1.76.20.24615 cannot use MySQL DB 9.4 as connexion storage backend ?
author: jeanbruder
body_snippet:
Dear mRemoteNG team,

As described in the documentation pages on using a database to save the configuration, I want to use MySQL. However, I am unable to connect to it. I have created a database named mRemoteNG and a user named mRemoteNG with full rights to this database. I can connect to it from a tool such as DBeaver, but not from mremoteng. Could you help me with this?

Thank you in advance, Jean

iterations.description: Issue is reported against legacy version 1.76.20 which is out of support; needs verification on current build before any fix can be scoped.
Bulk verified: issue reviewed and triaged

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: will check later, this version out of support but if i remember correctly it was some issues with correct table and need manualy run a script to fix it, but will check a bit later today, if its not production critical please try NB - > https://github.com/mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG/releases/tag/20251007-v1.78.2-NB-(3208)

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Update from fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`**

The current fork auto-creates MySQL schema and upgrades missing columns automatically. MySQL 9.x compatibility should be improved. Please try the latest **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**.

Fix included in **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**.

notes: Legacy v1.76.20 issue. Current fork auto-creates MySQL schema and upgrades missing columns.
